review_text: This film did not appeal to me when it was released. However, over the years it has grown on me. Last time, I actually enjoyed having Nicholson scare the bejesus out of me.

I rewatched this horror classic after many years and thoroughly enjoyed it more than I remembered. It’s a slow film, but it’s never boring, it keeps you on edge and builds suspense and dread the whole time with moments of expertly crafted horror throughout. The whole main cast is absolutely fantastic and portray convincing characters. One of Kubrick’s finest works.

My favorite movie of all time! The way this film is shot was truly genius, along with a more psychological horror approach than a jumpscare type. Although it's not fully faithful to the Steven King book, on its own it is truly a masterpiece than will never get old no matter how many times you watch it!
It's a horror clasic I love it so much I read the book and I've watched the movie it is so fun to watch a horror clasic and a gem to have

Every time I think about this movie let alone watch it, it seems and feels better. It has a beautiful build up with maximum horror during every stage. It genuinely gets horrifying using tactics and strategies used to this day. Easily one of the most well planned a strongly executed horrors to date.

This is one of the scariest movies I have ever seen. Jack Nicholson is simply outstanding and although fictional, there is a very realistic element that focuses on what cabin fever can do to the mind. The supernatural elements are frightening and superb and you have the infamous twin daughters who will leave an unforgettable imprint on the brain. If you are a fan of really good horror movies, not the corny type, this one is for you. In fact, I liked the movie more than the book for several reasons, but the book is outstanding 2. I love Stephen King but I found the movie to be even scarier than the book and that is not a jab at King as I have heard he did not like Kubrick’s movie adaptation of his novel very much and King has earned the right to say whatever he wants regarding his stories and movies based on his books, but this film is a horror classic and will rock you to the core. One of Nicholson’s best acting jobs ever and that’s saying a lot!
I got the chills down my spine. Terrific horror.
If you enjoy watching classic horror, movies, this is a must see!!😎👍

Watched it in 1983ish and it just seemed okay...Watched it today for the 2nd time ever and just did not find it interesting to the point I had to encourage and really push myself to finish it. Doesn't feel personal enough with character building or relationship the audience could or should feel with the cast. Not the biggest fan of Kubrick...I know, that's almost illegal to say. The best or most interesting scenes for me was the interview and the stair scene with the wife holding the bat. The most interesting character was by far Dick Hallorann. I should probably read the novel, but it'll have to take a different approach in order for me to finish it. On a scale of 1 - 10, 10 being the best, I would give it a 4.5...mostly for the look and the interests I have in the era.
This is a masterpiece of movie making. The critics calling it "slow" aren’t wrong, but it’s slow in such a magnificent way. Every scene, every frame, is genius.

Considering my age, I still absolutely love The Shining. It’s one of those rare films that proves great cinema doesn’t age—it simply changes with the audience. The acting is phenomenal, especially Jack Nicholson’s performance, which slowly unravels in a way that’s disturbing without relying on constant shock. The directing is just as impressive; every shot feels deliberate, from the wide, empty hallways to the quiet moments that allow tension to build naturally. The horror in The Shining isn’t loud or flashy. It’s slow, psychological, and deeply unsettling, driven by atmosphere rather than jump scares. By modern standards it may not seem traditionally scary, but that only strengthens it. The film trusts the audience to feel uncomfortable without being told when to be afraid. More than anything, The Shining is about isolation and madness. The Overlook Hotel feels alive, and that unease lingers long after the film ends. It’s not about fear—it’s about unforgettable entertainment.

远望酒店的空间结构将微缩的家庭内矛盾遽然放大，暴力手段的升级又反映出病态心理的加剧；个人的微观历史和国族的宏观历史在故事中闭合，前景中的伦理关系和背景中的种族关系共同构成了恐惧情绪的基础。暴雪封山所产生的封闭环境让最终的追杀与逃脱充满戏剧性，如今看来俨然是非对称竞技游戏的模样，这种跨媒体的溯源也颇为有趣。

But, overall, I give The Shining a 10/10 or a 5/5 stars.

The Shining starring Jack Nicholson is a psychological horror film that explores isolation, madness, and supernatural terror. Jack Torrance becomes the winter caretaker of the remote Overlook Hotel, where he slowly descends into insanity. Kubrick’s direction is precise, using long shots and eerie settings to build tension. Nicholson’s performance is intense and unforgettable, while Shelley Duvall and Danny Lloyd deliver convincing roles. The screenplay balances horror and psychological drama, letting suspense grow naturally. The cinematography, sound design, and hotel layout create a chilling atmosphere. Themes of family strain, addiction, and obsession resonate. Though not light viewing, The Shining remains a classic, influential, and iconic film in horror cinema, blending psychological depth with terrifying visuals and performances that leave a lasting impression.
A master class in building atmospheric suspense.

Movie is outdated but seeing it on the premium format IMAX in 2025 brings back why we love movies originally from the 70s , 80s, and 90s decades. To truly enjoy Kubrick's masterclass directorial efforts to bring Stephen King's novel onto the big screen , you have to enjoy the way it was filmed , the actors' performances and the haunting score. If you like classic horror and want to experience in the biggest picture format.... GO SEE THIS FILM
